fb_tickets.subject: Pipeline events not reaching Snowflake sink
fb_tickets.description: We configured a Snowflake sink for our production pipeline 3 days ago. Events are showing as processed in the dashboard but no data is appearing in our Snowflake tables. We've verified the Snowflake credentials and warehouse are correct.

fb_tickets.subject: Cannot create new environment - 500 error
fb_tickets.description: When clicking 'Create Environment' in workspace settings, we get a 500 Internal Server Error. This started happening after we upgraded to Team plan yesterday. Tried multiple browsers and incognito mode.

fb_tickets.subject: How to set up CDC for MySQL with SSL?
fb_tickets.description: We need to configure CDC from our MySQL database that requires SSL connections. The documentation shows the basic setup but doesn't cover SSL certificate configuration. Can you provide guidance?

fb_tickets.subject: Billing discrepancy on March invoice
fb_tickets.description: Our March invoice shows 8.2M events but our internal tracking shows only 6.1M. Can you provide a breakdown of event counts by date and pipeline route? We need this for our finance team's review.

fb_tickets.subject: Need help with onboarding - first pipeline setup
fb_tickets.description: We just signed up and are trying to set up our first CDC pipeline from PostgreSQL to BigQuery. The wizard is asking for replication settings but we're not sure what WAL level to use. Can someone walk us through?

fb_tickets.subject: Webhook delivery delays during peak hours
fb_tickets.description: Our webhook sink shows delivery delays of 30-60 seconds during peak hours (9-11 AM EST). Off-peak delivery is under 2 seconds. This is affecting our real-time alerting system. We're on Team plan.

fb_reviews.review_text: SchemaBounce replaced our entire Fivetran + dbt setup. The real-time CDC streaming is exactly what we needed for our fraud detection pipeline. Sub-second latency with zero maintenance overhead. The Kolumn IaC tool is a game changer for managing database schemas across environments.
fb_reviews.title: Best real-time data platform we've used
fb_reviews.response_text: Thank you Sarah! Great to hear about the fraud detection use case. We'd love to feature your story as a case study if you're open to it.

fb_reviews.review_text: Solid CDC platform with great real-time capabilities. Setup was straightforward and support was responsive. Only giving 4 stars because the documentation could use more examples for complex sink configurations. The Snowflake sink setup took some trial and error.
fb_reviews.title: Great platform, documentation needs work
fb_reviews.response_text: Thanks Mike! We hear you on the docs -- we're actively expanding our sink configuration guides. Expect more Snowflake examples by end of March.

fb_reviews.review_text: We evaluated Airbyte, Fivetran, and SchemaBounce. SchemaBounce won on latency (sub-second vs 5-15 min), price (event-based vs connector-based), and the fact that Kolumn is included free. Our data team manages everything through HCL now -- no more clicking through UIs.
fb_reviews.title: Blows away Fivetran and Airbyte on latency

fb_reviews.review_text: Interesting approach to CDC streaming. The agent-based data layer is promising but still early. Would love to see more pre-built integrations for SaaS tools like HubSpot and Salesforce. The community is growing and the team is very responsive.
fb_reviews.title: Promising platform with great potential
fb_reviews.response_text: Thanks David! HubSpot and Salesforce connectors are in our Q2 roadmap. Join our Discord for early access.

fb_reviews.review_text: The product concept is good but we ran into reliability issues. Our pipeline dropped events twice in the first month with no alerting. Support took 6 hours to respond to a critical issue. For a real-time platform, this is not acceptable.
fb_reviews.title: Reliability needs improvement
fb_reviews.response_text: Lisa, we sincerely apologize for the experience. We've since implemented dead letter queue alerting and improved our on-call response times. I'd love to connect directly to discuss how we can make this right -- please reach out to support@schemabounce.com.

fb_reviews.review_text: As a FinTech company, sub-second data latency is critical for us. SchemaBounce delivers. We process 50M+ events/month and the platform handles it without breaking a sweat. The pricing model (per event) is much more predictable than per-connector pricing.
fb_reviews.title: Essential for FinTech real-time needs

fb_nps_responses.feedback: The real-time CDC capabilities completely transformed our fraud detection pipeline. We went from 15-minute batch processing to sub-second detection. Would recommend to any FinTech company.
Good product but documentation could be better. Spent too long figuring out Snowflake sink configuration. Once it works though, it's solid.
We experienced two pipeline failures in our first month. For a platform that markets itself on reliability and real-time delivery, this was very disappointing. Support response times need to improve.
Switched from Fivetran and haven't looked back. The event-based pricing alone saves us $2K/month. Kolumn being free is the cherry on top.
Easy to get started. The onboarding support was great. Haven't fully explored the advanced features yet but initial impressions are very positive.

Essential infrastructure for our payment processing platform. 50M+ events/month processed reliably. The team is responsive and the product keeps getting better. Already recommended to two other companies.
Webhook delivery delays during peak hours are a problem for our alerting use case. The platform works well off-peak but real-time means real-time, not 30-60 seconds delayed.

fb_feature_requests.title: Salesforce CDC connector
fb_feature_requests.description: Native CDC connector for Salesforce that captures object changes in real-time without polling. Should support standard and custom objects with field-level change tracking.
fb_feature_requests.use_case: Our sales team needs real-time sync between Salesforce and our data warehouse for live pipeline dashboards. Current polling approach has 15-minute delay.

fb_feature_requests.title: Custom webhook transformation with JavaScript
fb_feature_requests.description: Allow users to define JavaScript transformation functions that run on webhook payloads before they're delivered to sinks. Should support field mapping, filtering, and enrichment.
fb_feature_requests.use_case: We receive webhooks from multiple sources with different schemas. We need to normalize them before they hit our data warehouse. Currently doing this with a Lambda function in between.

fb_feature_requests.title: Slack alerting for pipeline failures
fb_feature_requests.description: Send configurable Slack notifications when pipeline events fail, when error rates exceed thresholds, or when sinks become unreachable. Include event details and direct links to the affected pipeline.
fb_feature_requests.use_case: Our on-call team needs immediate Slack alerts when pipelines fail. Currently we only find out from downstream consumers complaining about missing data.

fb_feature_requests.title: Multi-region deployment support
fb_feature_requests.description: Support deploying pipeline infrastructure in multiple regions (US, EU, APAC) with data residency controls. Allow customers to choose where their data is processed and stored.
fb_feature_requests.use_case: GDPR requires us to process EU customer data within the EU. We need to run separate pipelines in EU and US regions with guaranteed data residency.

fb_feature_requests.title: GraphQL API for pipeline management
fb_feature_requests.description: Provide a GraphQL API alongside the REST API for more flexible querying of pipeline configurations, metrics, and status. Should support subscriptions for real-time updates.
fb_feature_requests.use_case: We build internal developer portals and GraphQL makes it much easier to compose exactly the data views we need without multiple REST calls.

fb_feature_requests.title: Dead letter queue self-service redrive UI
fb_feature_requests.description: Allow customers to view, inspect, and redrive failed events from the dead letter queue through the dashboard UI without needing to contact support.
fb_feature_requests.use_case: When events fail (bad schema, sink timeout), we need to fix the issue and replay the events ourselves. Currently this requires a support ticket and manual intervention.
